text: The custom was to accompany someone who was departing on a long journey for part of the initial journey, for example, to the outskirts of the next city, or to main road some distance from the point of departure, etc.
The word ‘fiant’ is the plural of the word ‘fiat’: let it be.
The word ‘anathema’ means ‘cut off’ or ‘excommunicated’ or ‘accursed.’
